summary: iter179 frontend testing of /orders Fix-Routing wrench UI for owner. Login as admin (hanialdujaili@gmail.com) succeeded; /orders page renders correctly. Seeded 1 sync order via POST /api/sync/orders with is_offline_order=true and original_order_number=14. The DB stored is_offline_order=true correctly, BUT the GET /api/orders response strips both is_offline_order AND original_order_number because the Pydantic OrderResponse model (server.py:1547-1589) whitelists fields and ConfigDict(extra='ignore') drops them. As a result, the Wrench button conditional `(order.is_offline_order || order.original_order_number)` in Orders.js:606 NEVER evaluates true, so the fix-routing wrench button is NOT rendered in the DOM (verified: data-testid^=fix-routing- count = 0 while order #24 is visible on screen). Frontend wiring (state, dialog markup, handlers, data-testid attributes, auto-inference logic when payment_method=delivery_company) all look correct on code review — they cannot be exercised end-to-end until backend exposes the two flag fields.

critical.issue: OrderResponse Pydantic model (server.py:1547-1589) is missing fields `is_offline_order: Optional[bool]` and `original_order_number: Optional[int]`. With model_config = ConfigDict(extra='ignore'), FastAPI silently drops these from the response even though they exist in the Mongo doc. This is the ROOT CAUSE: the wrench button never appears for offline orders even when the data is correct. FIX: add the two fields (and likely also `delivery_company_*`, `customer_type`, `paid_amount`, `routing_fixed_at`, `routing_fix_history` so the dialog can show full state) to OrderResponse.

OfflineOrder Pydantic model (routes/sync_routes.py:32-73) does NOT include `original_order_number` field, so when client sends it, it is discarded before persistence. Verified: sent original_order_number=14, DB stored None. The renumbering job (server.py:18830) writes this field via direct $set, bypassing Pydantic — so existing renumbered orders DO have it in DB, but newly-synced offline orders cannot carry it through this endpoint.

ui_bugs.issue: Button at line 606 with data-testid=`fix-routing-${order.id}` is never rendered because backend OrderResponse strips is_offline_order/original_order_number. UI code itself is correct.

integration_issues.issue: Cannot be exercised end-to-end. With order seeded (is_offline_order=true in DB), the /api/orders payload omits the flag, so Orders.js conditional fails, wrench never appears, dialog never opens, PATCH /api/sync/orders/{id}/fix-routing is unreachable from the UI.

action_items: MAIN AGENT (backend, REQUIRED to unblock UI): add `is_offline_order: Optional[bool] = False` and `original_order_number: Optional[int] = None` to OrderResponse in /app/backend/server.py:1547. Also recommend adding `customer_type`, `delivery_company_id`, `delivery_company_name`, `delivery_company_order_id`, `paid_amount`, `routing_fixed_at`, `routing_fixed_by`, `routing_fix_history` so the fix-routing dialog can pre-populate accurately and badges reflect updated state.

MAIN AGENT (backend): add `original_order_number: Optional[int] = None` to OfflineOrder model in /app/backend/routes/sync_routes.py:32-73 AND map it into new_order dict (~line 224) so future renumbered offline syncs persist this field via the public API.

RETEST (frontend): once both backend fixes ship, retest full flow — login → /orders → wrench visible on offline order → click → dialog opens with pre-populated values → change payment_method to delivery_company → verify auto-set customer_type=delivery_company + payment_status=unpaid + delivery-company section appears (testids fix-delivery-company, fix-delivery-company-order-id) → save → toast 'تم تصحيح مسار الطلب' → dialog closes → list reflects new payment_status badge. Also verify cashier user does NOT see wrench.
critical_code_review_comments: Orders.js:606 — conditional `(order.is_offline_order || order.original_order_number)` is correct in intent but brittle: if backend ever returns 0 or empty string for original_order_number it would still hide the button. Consider explicit `order.is_offline_order === true || (order.original_order_number != null)`.

Orders.js:269-275 — submitFixRouting clears delivery fields when order_type !== 'delivery', but does NOT clear them when payment_method changes away from delivery_company. Edge case: user picks delivery_company → fills company name → switches back to cash. Fields are cleaned only by the order_type check, so delivery_company_name persists in the payload. Backend will store it. Recommend: also null out delivery_company_* if payment_method !== 'delivery_company'.

Orders.js:727-740 — payment_method onValueChange auto-sets customer_type and payment_status. Good. But when user later manually changes payment_status (e.g., to 'partial'), the next payment_method change will overwrite their manual choice silently. Acceptable, but a small toast hint would help.

Orders.js:78 (~line 78 conceptually, actually near 605) — wrench button uses `border-amber-500 text-amber-600 hover:bg-amber-50`; on dark theme the hover bg-amber-50 reads almost-white which is fine, but the static state's amber-600 on the dark navbar-style row may have low contrast. Minor design feedback.

Backend OrderResponse uses ConfigDict(extra='ignore') which silently drops any DB field not declared in the model. This is dangerous for evolving schemas — every new feature must remember to update OrderResponse. Consider switching to extra='allow' for read-only response models, OR add an explicit allow-list for newly added fields like is_offline_order, original_order_number, routing_fix_history.

success_rate.backend: regression skipped per request (iter178 covered it)
success_rate.frontend: 0% end-to-end (wrench never renders due to backend response gap); UI code review 100% correct

test_credentials: Admin: hanialdujaili@gmail.com / Hani@2024 (used). Cashier role test not performed — blocked by primary issue.
seed_data_creation: Created 1 sync order id=multi-tenant-pos-28 (offline_id=TEST_iter179_offline_*, customer_name='TEST iter179 wrong-routing', total=12000, is_offline_order=True in DB) — DELETED in cleanup at end of run.
context_for_next_testing_agent: After main agent adds is_offline_order + original_order_number to OrderResponse, just login as admin, seed one sync order via POST /api/sync/orders with is_offline_order=true, navigate to /orders for today's date, and verify the wrench button is visible. The dialog markup itself is solid (verified by code read of Orders.js:698-846) — every required data-testid is present: fix-routing-dialog, fix-order-type, fix-payment-method, fix-payment-status, fix-customer-type, fix-delivery-company, fix-delivery-company-order-id, fix-routing-cancel, fix-routing-save. Also note: the orders list filters by today's date by default with no date picker, so always seed an order created today (created_at = now) for UI testing. POST /api/sync/orders correctly sets created_at to now if omitted.
rca of the issue: Symptom: wrench fix-routing button does not appear on /orders page for any order. Reproduction: (1) login admin (2) POST /api/sync/orders with is_offline_order=true → 200 + DB stores is_offline_order=true (verified via direct mongo query) (3) GET /api/orders?date=today → returns the order BUT response payload omits is_offline_order and original_order_number keys entirely (verified via in-browser fetch). Root cause: OrderResponse Pydantic model in /app/backend/server.py:1547-1589 does not declare these fields and uses extra='ignore', so FastAPI strips them from the serialized response. The frontend Orders.js:606 conditional therefore evaluates falsy and the wrench is never rendered. Mitigation: add both fields to OrderResponse. Also add original_order_number to OfflineOrder input model so the public sync endpoint can persist it (currently only the internal renumbering job sets it via direct $set).
